programmes.show_description: Christmas is a time to be with the family, but not for Alf Garnett. He is destined to spend Christmas in hospital, having his hip replaced. In Sickness and In Health Christmas Special 1987
With Dusty Springfield, Elvis Costello and The Police. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
Simon settles an account with a corrupt American attorney, using his own methods of justice to do so. S1 E8: The Element of Doubt
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
By 1915 trench warfare had stalled, Naval and amphibious plans had failed disastrously, revealing why the Dardanelles campaign collapsed. Narrated by Robert Powell.
At the outbreak of WWII, the only heavy four-engined bombers in the war were either Russian or American - soon the Short Stirling, the Avro Lancaster, and the Liberator were added. Ep6: Heavy Bombers
After Hitler invaded Poland, the Western Allies and Nazi Germany were technically at war - for 7 months, neither side engaged in major military land operations. S1 E7: 'Sitzkrieg' and the Phoney War
One of the world's elite WWII fighting forces, the Commandos were created by Churchill in the summer of 1940 as a means of striking at the coasts of Nazi-occupied Europe. Ep7: The Paras and Commandos
The Hurricane will be remembered for the vital role it played in WWII, and how it destroyed more enemy aircraft during the Battle of Britain than all other air and ground defences combined. S3 E1
Japan begins to conquer southeast Asia as the Allies attempt to block the empire's aggressive expansion, but ruthless tactics by Japan only help to further their war effort. Ep6: Road to Burma

We examine the course of savage fighting in the Balkans in the latter half of the conflict in World War 2 where after the uprising by the Romanians, the Soviet army moved swiftly to capture Bucharest.
Examining the history of war tactics. Urban warfare is the most vicious form of fighting and takes a soldier back to the most primitive type of warfare: hand-to-hand. S1 E13
The story of RJ Mitchell and his team who developed the Spitfire just in time to defeat the Nazi attacks during the Battle of Britain. Ep2: The Man Who Designed The Spitfire
A look into the lives of the rich and famous - this time we look at Oprah Winfrey, America's first lady of celebrity.
...You? There are some news events that change the world - we take you back to the 26th of December, 2004 and the Indonesian tsunami.
The evolution of the Vietnam conflict from a regional military engagement to an ever-expanding war, here exploring how it was logistically possible and a look at the Huey helicopter and its role.
Investigations reveal the greatest scuttle and act of defiance in wartime history as testimonies and CGI illuminate the events and subsequent salvage. Ep7: The Lost Fleet of WWI
The union head office wants Jack to force Colefax - a local employer - to allow it into his factory, but Jack has split loyalties; also, Bella is taken ill. S2 E12: In the Front Line You Get Shot At
Arthur's chum Sidney comes to him for protection from a murderer, but Arthur's idea of a safe house is a caravan belonging to Dave's sister. S10 E3: All Things Brighton Beautiful
Ross attempts to learn of Dwight's fate in Revolutionary France. In Cornwall, George and Elizabeth lock horns with Sam and Drake. Starring Aidan Turner. S3 E3
Drama series following the exploits of old-school detective Gene Hunt from the drama series Life on Mars. DI Alex Drake is shot and lands in 1981 before coming face-to-face with Hunt. S1 E1
Can our patriotic and unromantic hero be contemplating a trip down under and marriage to Mrs Hollingbery, the woman he loves to hate? It seems so - but there is, of course, an ulterior motive! S4 E1
With David Essex and Ellen Foley. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
Simon goes fishing with a glamorous girl - and catches a much bigger net of fish than he expects. S1 E9: The Effete Angler

Amelia Earhart inspired millions, then vanished on her Pacific flight. Her fate remains one of aviation's enduring mysteries. Narrated by Robert Powell.
Battleships were seen as the key weapon at sea at the beginning of World War II, but their vulnerability was exposed by submarines and aircraft over the next six years. Ep7: The Battleship
As Britain alone faced German forces with Nazi troops storming across Europe at great speed, the course of the war was far from certain. S1 E8: The Battle of Britain
The Norwegian resistance movement was one of the most effective movements in the whole of occupied Europe; its activities helped destroy Germany's warships. Ep8: The Norwegian Resistance Fighters
The Ju 52 is a German transport aircraft that delivered the attacking forces and their supplies during the German invasion of Norway, Denmark, France and the Low Countries in WWII. S3 E2
The war takes root in Africa as an ambitious Mussolini expands the war into North Africa; he sends ill-prepared soldiers and forces Hitler to come to his ally's aid. Ep7: Desert War
Tim Pigott-Smith narrates the story of the battle for Guadalcanal and the capture of this strategically crucial island in World War 2 during which more than 25,000 Japanese lost their lives.
Examining the history of war tactics. Breaking a fortified line is the battleplan every soldier fears. It means attacking defences head-on and it's going to be a bloodbath. S2 E1
The story of Colonel Count Claus von Stauffenberg and the other German officers who tried to kill Adolf Hitler in a bomb plot of July 1944. Ep3: The Man Who Stood Up To Hitler
A look into the lives of the rich and famous - this time we take a look at the dramatic rise to stardom of the one and only Angelina Jolie.
...Were You? There are some news events that change the world - we take you back to some of Northern Ireland's darkest days and the journey towards lasting peace that was agreed on Good Friday 1998.
We examine the Vietnam War from the air, water and land along with those that fought look at the importance of reconnaissance in the war effort and the role of the American navy in Vietnam.
Investigations into three historical collisions including the HMS Curacoa, which sank during WWII after colliding with the Queen Mary. Ep8: Collisions at Sea
Raw materials essential to keep the Nazis fighting came through Bordeaux before Royal Marine Major 'Blondie' Hasler sent a special raiding force in canoes. Ep5: Cockleshell Raiders
Jack steps down as secretary for the union, and everybody is upset about the potential demolition of the country house Mandrake Place. S2 E13: The Simple Pleasures of the Rich
Arthur decides to rent out Bert and Doreen's house while the pair are on holiday, but hasn't realised that his new tenant is in trouble with the police. S10 E4: One Flew Over The Parents Nest
George proves merciless as the new magistrate, and Clowance is christened. Ross discovers the means to both help Dwight and combat Cornwall's famine. Starring Aidan Turner. S3 E4
Drama series following the exploits of Life on Mars's DCI Gene Hunt. A royal wedding looms and it's up to Gene and a restless Alex to keep the streets quiet. S1 E2
